issue_title: [OID4VCI] Revisit and fix OAuthClient.credentialOfferRequest()
issue_body:
### Description

Currently we send a CredentialOffer request like this ...

```
        CredentialOfferResponse credentialOfferResponse = oauth.oid4vc()
                .credentialOfferRequest()
                .endpoint(offerUri)
                .send();
        int statusCode = credentialOfferResponse.getStatusCode();
        if (HttpStatus.SC_OK != statusCode) {
            throw new IllegalStateException(credentialOfferResponse.getErrorDescription() != null
                    ? credentialOfferResponse.getErrorDescription()
                    : "Request failed with status " + statusCode);
        }
        CredentialsOffer credOffer = credentialOfferResponse.getCredentialsOffer();
```

or 

```
        CredentialOfferResponse credentialOfferResponse = oauth.oid4vc()
                .credentialOfferRequest()
                .nonce(credOfferUri.getNonce())
                .send();
        int statusCode = credentialOfferResponse.getStatusCode();
        if (HttpStatus.SC_OK != statusCode) {
            throw new IllegalStateException(credentialOfferResponse.getErrorDescription() != null
                    ? credentialOfferResponse.getErrorDescription()
                    : "Request failed with status " + statusCode);
        }

```

This code has several issues ...

* It (again often) relies on constructing the endpoint Url externally
* From a Wallet perspective - it has the complete offerUrl (nonce is a Keycloak proprietary thing)
* From our test API perspective we have the CredentialOfferURI, which can be used directly
* error handling should not have to be duplicated for every request


### Value Proposition

* use credential offer url or CredentialOfferURI directly
* error handling by CredentialOfferResponse

### Goals

Cleaner and more reliable code

### Non-Goals

--

### Discussion

_No response_

### Notes

_No response_
commit_message:
[OID4VCI] Revisit and fix OAuthClient.credentialOfferRequest()

Signed-off-by: Thomas Diesler <tdiesler@ibm.com>

issue_title: Missing anti-ID phishing check for getting client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

For the Admin API v1, there is this check in the `ClientsResource`:

```java
ClientModel clientModel = realm.getClientById(id);
        if (clientModel == null) {
            // we do this to make sure somebody can't phish ids
            if (auth.clients().canList()) throw new NotFoundException("Could not find client");
            else throw new ForbiddenException();
        }
```

It means that if there's no `clients().canList()` permission, the 403 should be returned instead of 404. 

We're missing this check in the Admin V2 API

### Version

999

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Returns 403 when the permission is not granted

### Actual behavior

Always returns 404

### How to Reproduce?

Try to obtain a non-existent user without the `client().canList()` permission via the Client Admin API v2

issue_title: Duplicate `{client-uuid}` path parameter in OpenAPI spec
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The endpoint `GET /admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client-uuid}/roles/{role-name}/composites/clients/{client-uuid}` contains two path parameters with the same name `{client-uuid}`. This produces an invalid OpenAPI 3.0 specification.

Per the [OpenAPI 3.0 specification](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#path-templating), path parameter names must be unique within a single path template.

### Version

26.5.2 (and main branch)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The OpenAPI specification should have unique path parameter names, allowing code generators to produce valid client code.

### Actual behavior

The generated OpenAPI spec contains duplicate `{client-uuid}` parameters in the path, causing OpenAPI code generators to fail or produce incorrect client code.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Download the OpenAPI spec from https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/26.5.2/rest-api/openapi.yaml
2. Attempt to generate a Go client using [oapi-codegen](https://github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen)
3. The code generator fails with:
   ```
   error generating code: error creating operation definitions: path '/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client-uuid}/roles/{role-name}/composites/clients/{client-uuid}' has 4 positional parameters, but spec has 3 declared
   ```

### Root cause

`RoleContainerResource.java` uses `{client-uuid}` in its `@Path` annotation at line 427:

```java
@Path("{role-name}/composites/clients/{client-uuid}")
```

When this resource is mounted under `ClientResource` (which contributes `{client-uuid}` to the path via `ClientsResource`), the full path ends up with duplicate parameter names:

```
/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client-uuid}/roles/{role-name}/composites/clients/{client-uuid}
```

## Anything else?

**Proposed fix:** Rename `{client-uuid}` to `{targetClientUuid}` in `RoleContainerResource.java`.

`{clientUuid}` is not sufficient here because most code generators normalise `{client-uuid}` (from the parent path) to `clientUuid` as well, which would still produce a collision. `{targetClientUuid}` makes the semantics clear: it refers to the client whose roles are being queried as composites, not the client that owns the role.

This is a non-breaking change because:
- The actual URL path segment value doesn't change (it's still a UUID)
- Only the OpenAPI parameter name changes
commit_message:
Fix duplicate client-uuid path parameter in OpenAPI spec

Rename {client-uuid} to {targetClientUuid} in RoleContainerResource to
avoid duplicate parameter names when the resource is mounted under
ClientResource. {clientUuid} is not sufficient because code generators
normalise {client-uuid} from the parent path to clientUuid as well.

Closes #46015

Signed-off-by: Zorian Motso <zorianmotso@gmail.com>

issue_title: [OIDC4VCI] - Types in JWT_VC
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area oid4vc ### Describe the bug Good morning, We believe we have identified a possible issue when building the Verifiable Credential, specifically in the construction of the `type` part of the credential. ### Version 26.5.2 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The expected behavior is that the type attribute of the credential contains the values specified in vc.supported_credential_types, appearing as follows: ``` "type": [ "VerifiableCredential", "VerifiableAttestation", "DoctorID" ] ``` ### Actual behavior We have configured a scope that contains the following values in vc.supported_credential_types: "VerifiableCredential,VerifiableAttestation,DoctorID", and when checking the .well-known endpoint, it is returned correctly: ```json "credential_definition": { "type": [ "VerifiableCredential", "VerifiableAttestation", "DoctorID" ] } ``` However, when the credential is actually constructed, the type field is set incorrectly. ### How to Reproduce? Add a scope containing the attribute: `"vc.supported_credential_types": "VerifiableCredential,VerifiableAttestation,DoctorID"` with the expected values, and verify on the Keycloak web interface that they have been correctly set. Additionally, you can also check this by calling the .well-known/openid-credential-issuer endpoint, where the credential_definition shows the types. However, when you complete the process of building the credential and obtain the resulting Verifiable Credential, checking the types values reveals that different values have been set—values that do not correspond to those configured in the scope. ### Anything else? I also believe that the values for the issuanceDate and expirationDate attributes: ``` "issuanceDate": 1770285478.882, "expirationDate": 1801821478.882 ``` are not correct and should be formatted in ISO 8601 format. (2025-06-13T12:04:42.200515285Z)

issue_title: Revisit retransmission metrics documentation
issue_body:
### Description

The statement about the [healthy cluster having zero retransmissions](https://www.keycloak.org/observability/metrics-for-troubleshooting-clustering-and-network#_retransmissions)  is not 100% correct.
`UNICAST3` (protocol response for message reliable delivery) delays sending acks to reduce the network traffic. It may happen, due to timing issues, that the sender has not received an ack for a while and retransmits the message eagerly. It doesn't mean there is a problem with the cluster.
commit_message:

Closes #46059

issue_title: "Full scope allowed" toggle reverts to previous state when switching between Scope and Mappers tabs in Dedicated scopes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the **Clients → Client details → Client scopes → Dedicated scopes** screen, when the user toggles the "Full Scope Allowed" switch (e.g., from ON → OFF) on the Scope tab, then navigates to the Mappers tab and returns back to the Scope tab, the toggle visually reverts to its previous state (ON in this example).

The change **is persisted on the server side**, a full page refresh or navigating away and returning to the Dedicated scopes screen shows the correct (updated) value. This is a **UI-only stale state issue**, likely caused by the component re-reading a cached/stale value when the Scope tab is re-mounted after tab switching.

### Version

26.5.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

After toggling "Full Scope Allowed" on the Scope tab and switching to the Mappers tab and back, the Scope tab should reflect the updated toggle state without requiring a full page refresh.

### Actual behavior

The "Full Scope Allowed" toggle visually reverts to its previous state when the user navigates away from the Scope tab (to Mappers) and then returns. A full page reload is required to see the correct state.


### How to Reproduce?

1. Navigate to **Clients → (select any client) → Client scopes tab → (click the client's dedicated scope link).**
2. On the **Scope** tab, note the current state of the **"Full scope allowed"** toggle (e.g., ON).
3. Toggle it to the opposite state (e.g., OFF). The change is saved.
4. Switch to the **Mappers** tab.
5. Switch back to the **Scope** tab.
6. **Observe**: The "Full scope allowed" toggle shows the old state (ON), not the updated state (OFF).
7. **Refresh the page (F5)**: The toggle now correctly shows the updated state (OFF).

### Anything else?

A screen recording demonstrating the issue is attached below.
The bug is cosmetic/UI-only; the server-side state is correctly updated after the toggle. The problem is that the Scope tab component displays stale data after a tab switch.

This could be caused by the tab component not invalidating or refetching the client scope data when the Scope tab is re-mounted/re-activated.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/950d3f1c-d68b-4f91-8f97-385f3c460c9d
commit_message:
Sync client scope state between tabs in dedicated scopes

Closes #46083

issue_title: Newly created resource appears on both current and next page in Authorization Resources tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the **Clients → Client details → Authorization → Resources** tab, when the page size is set to 10 and there are already 10 resources (filling page 1), creating a new (11th) resource causes pagination issues. The newly created resource is appended to page 1 (making it show 11 items), and the resource that was already at the bottom of page 1 also appears on page 2 as a duplicate.

This is a **UI-only stale state / client-side list management issue**. The server-side data is correct at all times. The problem is that the client-side resource list optimistically appends the new item to the current page's array without respecting the page size limit, and does not refetch the paginated list after the creation, causing existing items to leak across page boundaries.

### Version

26.5.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a new resource is created and the current page is full (e.g., 10 items with page size 10), the new resource should only appear on the next page (page 2). The current page should remain at 10 items, or the list should be refetched to reflect the correct paginated state.

### Actual behavior

Creating the 11th resource causes it to be appended to page 1 (showing 11 items instead of 10). Additionally, the resource that was already at the bottom of page 1 also appears on page 2 as a duplicate entry across pages.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Navigate to **Clients → (select a client with Authorization enabled) → Authorization → Resources** tab.
2. Create **10 resources** so that page 1 is completely filled (with page size set to 10).
3. Create an **11th resource**.
4. **Observe**: The newly created resource appears at the bottom of page 1 (which now shows 11 items instead of 10).
5. Navigate to **page 2**.
6. **Observe**: The resource that was at the bottom of page 1 also appears on page 2 (duplicate).
7. **Refresh the page (F5)**: The resource list now correctly shows 10 items on page 1 and 1 item on page 2.

### Anything else?

A screen recording demonstrating the issue is attached below.

The bug is cosmetic/UI-only; the server-side state is correctly updated after the create operation. The problem is that the admin console's client-side list/pagination state is not properly invalidated or recalculated after a create operation that crosses a pagination boundary.

This could be caused by:
- The resource list component optimistically appending newly created items to the current page's array without checking the page size limit.
- Missing list refetch or cache invalidation after the create operation in the Authorization Resources tab.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/afd5bb50-dbe9-4bcf-bba5-0590c69869f9
commit_message:
Fix duplicated resource on current and next page in Auth Resources (#47446)

The Authorization Resources table was incorrectly duplicating the last
element of the current page as the first element of the next page.

This occurred because the fetching logic requests 'max + 1' items to
detect if a subsequent page exists. However, the UI was rendering the
entire array instead of limiting the display to the 'max' value.

Fixed by applying a .slice(0, max) before mapping the resources to
table rows. Added a Playwright E2E regression test to ensure unique
resource distribution across pages and prevent future regressions.

Closes #46088

---------

Signed-off-by: André Rocha <andre.queiroz.rocha@tecnico.ulisboa.pt>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>

issue_title: Deleting a resource on page 2 shows "No resources found" empty state while resources still exist in Authorization Resources tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue
- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.
### Area
admin/ui
### Describe the bug
In the **Clients → Client details → Authorization → Resources** tab, when the user is on page 2 of the paginated resource list and deletes a resource, the UI displays a **"No resources found"** empty state on page 2, even though resources still exist on page 1. A full page refresh (F5) is required to restore the correct state.

This is a **UI-only stale state issue**. The server-side data is correct at all times. The problem is that the delete handler removes the item from the current page's local array and, when the array becomes empty, shows the empty state without checking whether resources exist on other pages or navigating back to page 1.
### Version
26.5.2
### Regression
- [x] The issue is a regression
### Expected behavior
When a resource is deleted from page 2 and no items remain on that page, the UI should either:
- Navigate back to page 1 (which still has resources), or
- Refetch the list and display the correct paginated state.

It should never show "No resources found" while resources actually exist in the system.
### Actual behavior
Deleting the last (or only) resource on page 2 causes the UI to display "No resources found" empty state, even though page 1 still contains resources. A full page reload (F5) is required to see the correct state.
### How to Reproduce?
1. Navigate to **Clients → (select a client with Authorization enabled) → Authorization → Resources** tab.
2. Ensure there are **11 resources** (10 on page 1, 1 on page 2 with page size 10).
3. Navigate to **page 2**.
4. Delete the resource shown on page 2.
5. **Observe**: The UI shows "No resources found" on page 2, which is incorrect — 10 resources still exist on page 1.
6. **Refresh the page (F5)**: The resource list correctly shows 10 items on page 1.
### Anything else?
A screen recording demonstrating the issue is attached below.

The bug is cosmetic/UI-only; the server-side state is correctly updated after the delete operation. The problem is that the admin console's client-side pagination state is not properly recalculated after a delete operation that empties the current page.

This could be caused by:
- The delete handler removing the item from the local array and rendering the empty state based solely on the current page's array length, without considering total resource count across all pages.
- Missing list refetch, cache invalidation, or page navigation logic after delete operations in the Authorization Resources tab.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/887b5c90-9a9b-4886-9600-f60e0dcce258
commit_message:
Fix stale empty state after deleting last resource on a page (#47373)

When the last item on a paginated table is deleted, the UI
incorrectly displayed 'No resources found' instead of navigating
back to the previous page. This commit resolves the local state
issue and adds a Playwright E2E regression test to ensure the
table correctly refetches and displays the remaining resources.

Refactored E2E test:
- Replaced while(true) loop with a deterministic for loop;
- Implemented exact string matching and test id locators;
- Added resource cleanup to prevent polluting subsequent tests;
- Added deleteResource in AdminClient.ts to assist in the cleanup.

Closes #46089

Signed-off-by: João Viegas <joao.manuel.loja.viegas@tecnico.ulisboa.pt>

issue_title: Makes Database Query on Every Login Page Load Instead of Using Cache
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Keycloak Quarkus distribution performs a database query to the realm table with a join to realm_attributes on every login page load, despite having local cache configured. 

### Version

26.5.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When accessing the login page, Keycloak should retrieve realm configuration from the local cache (Infinispan) after the first request. Subsequent requests should not hit the database.

According to the [Keycloak caching documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/server/caching), realms should be cached by default:

### Actual behavior

Every login page request results in a database query:

```sql
select re1_0.ID,re1_0.ACCESS_CODE_LIFESPAN,re1_0.LOGIN_LIFESPAN,re1_0.USER_ACTION_LIFESPAN,re1_0.ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFESPAN,re1_0.ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFE_IMPLICIT,re1_0.ACCOUNT_THEME,re1_0.ADMIN_EVENTS_DETAILS_ENABLED,re1_0.ADMIN_EVENTS_ENABLED,re1_0.ADMIN_THEME,re1_0.ALLOW_USER_MANAGED_ACCESS,re1_0.BROWSER_FLOW,re1_0.CLIENT_AUTH_FLOW,re1_0.DEFAULT_LOCALE,re1_0.DEFAULT_ROLE,re1_0.DIRECT_GRANT_FLOW,re1_0.DOCKER_AUTH_FLOW,re1_0.DUPLICATE_EMAILS_ALLOWED,re1_0.EDIT_USERNAME_ALLOWED,re1_0.EMAIL_THEME,re1_0.ENABLED,re1_0.EVENTS_ENABLED,re1_0.EVENTS_EXPIRATION,re1_0.INTERNATIONALIZATION_ENABLED,re1_0.LOGIN_THEME,re1_0.LOGIN_WITH_EMAIL_ALLOWED,re1_0.MASTER_ADMIN_CLIENT,re1_0.NAME,re1_0.NOT_BEFORE,re1_0.OFFLINE_SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_ALG,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_DIGITS,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_COUNTER,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_WINDOW,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_PERIOD,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_TYPE,re1_0.PASSWORD_POLICY,re1_0.REFRESH_TOKEN_MAX_REUSE,re1_0.REGISTRATION_ALLOWED,re1_0.REG_EMAIL_AS_USERNAME,re1_0.REGISTRATION_FLOW,re1_0.REMEMBER_ME,re1_0.RESET_CREDENTIALS_FLOW,re1_0.RESET_PASSWORD_ALLOWED,re1_0.REVOKE_REFRESH_TOKEN,re1_0.SSL_REQUIRED,re1_0.SSO_IDLE_TIMEOUT,re1_0.SSO_IDLE_TIMEOUT_REMEMBER_ME,re1_0.SSO_MAX_LIFESPAN,re1_0.SSO_MAX_LIFESPAN_REMEMBER_ME,re1_0.VERIFY_EMAIL,a1_0.REALM_ID,a1_0.NAME,a1_0.VALUE from REALM re1_0 left join REALM_ATTRIBUTE a1_0 on re1_0.ID=a1_0.REALM_ID where re1_0.ID=?
```

This happens even with:

- Default cache configuration
- No custom SPI implementations
- Single realm setup
- No recent configuration changes

### How to Reproduce?

## 1. Project Setup

### Dockerfile

````text
FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest AS builder

# Enable health and metrics support
ENV KC_HEALTH_ENABLED=true
ENV KC_METRICS_ENABLED=true

# Configure a database vendor
ENV KC_DB=postgres

# Custom settings
COPY resources/keycloak/quarkus.properties /opt/keycloak/conf

WORKDIR /opt/keycloak
# for demonstration purposes only, please make sure to use proper certificates in production instead
RUN keytool -genkeypair -storepass password -storetype PKCS12 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -dname "CN=server" -alias server -ext "SAN:c=DNS:localhost,IP:127.0.0.1" -keystore conf/server.keystore
RUN /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build

FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
COPY --from=builder /opt/keycloak/ /opt/keycloak/

ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh"]
````

### docker-compose.yml

````yaml
version: "3.8"
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16.2
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: keycloak
      POSTGRES_USER: user
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
  keycloak:
    container_name: keycloak
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    command: ["start", "--optimized"]
    user: "1000:1000"
    environment:
      KC_DB_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/keycloak
      KC_DB_USERNAME: user
      KC_DB_PASSWORD: password
      KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME: admin
      KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin
      KC_LOG_ASYNC: "true"
      KC_HTTP_ENABLED: "true"
      KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT: "false"
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
      - "9000:9000"
      - "5005:5005"
    depends_on:
      - postgres

````

### Configuration File (resources/keycloak/quarkus.properties)

````properties
# Hibernate
quarkus.log.category."org.hibernate.SQL".level=DEBUG
````

## 2. Start the Environment

````bash
docker-compose up --build
````

Wait for Keycloak to initialize (check logs with docker-compose logs -f keycloak).

## 3. Reproduce the Issue

1. Access the Keycloak Admin Console: http://localhost:8080
2. Monitor the Keycloak logs: docker-compose logs -f keycloak

## 4. Observed Behavior

For every page load/refresh in the login page, you will see the following SQL query in the logs:

````text
2026-02-07 21:45:53,180 DEBUG [org.hibernate.SQL] (executor-thread-6) select re1_0.ID,re1_0.ACCESS_CODE_LIFESPAN,re1_0.LOGIN_LIFESPAN,re1_0.USER_ACTION_LIFESPAN,re1_0.ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFESPAN,re1_0.ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFE_IMPLICIT,re1_0.ACCOUNT_THEME,re1_0.ADMIN_EVENTS_DETAILS_ENABLED,re1_0.ADMIN_EVENTS_ENABLED,re1_0.ADMIN_THEME,re1_0.ALLOW_USER_MANAGED_ACCESS,re1_0.BROWSER_FLOW,re1_0.CLIENT_AUTH_FLOW,re1_0.DEFAULT_LOCALE,re1_0.DEFAULT_ROLE,re1_0.DIRECT_GRANT_FLOW,re1_0.DOCKER_AUTH_FLOW,re1_0.DUPLICATE_EMAILS_ALLOWED,re1_0.EDIT_USERNAME_ALLOWED,re1_0.EMAIL_THEME,re1_0.ENABLED,re1_0.EVENTS_ENABLED,re1_0.EVENTS_EXPIRATION,re1_0.INTERNATIONALIZATION_ENABLED,re1_0.LOGIN_THEME,re1_0.LOGIN_WITH_EMAIL_ALLOWED,re1_0.MASTER_ADMIN_CLIENT,re1_0.NAME,re1_0.NOT_BEFORE,re1_0.OFFLINE_SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_ALG,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_DIGITS,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_COUNTER,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_WINDOW,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_PERIOD,re1_0.OTP_POLICY_TYPE,re1_0.PASSWORD_POLICY,re1_0.REFRESH_TOKEN_MAX_REUSE,re1_0.REGISTRATION_ALLOWED,re1_0.REG_EMAIL_AS_USERNAME,re1_0.REGISTRATION_FLOW,re1_0.REMEMBER_ME,re1_0.RESET_CREDENTIALS_FLOW,re1_0.RESET_PASSWORD_ALLOWED,re1_0.REVOKE_REFRESH_TOKEN,re1_0.SSL_REQUIRED,re1_0.SSO_IDLE_TIMEOUT,re1_0.SSO_IDLE_TIMEOUT_REMEMBER_ME,re1_0.SSO_MAX_LIFESPAN,re1_0.SSO_MAX_LIFESPAN_REMEMBER_ME,re1_0.VERIFY_EMAIL,a1_0.REALM_ID,a1_0.NAME,a1_0.VALUE from REALM re1_0 left join REALM_ATTRIBUTE a1_0 on re1_0.ID=a1_0.REALM_ID where re1_0.ID=?
````

## 5. Expected vs Actual

- Expected: This query should appear only on the first request, with subsequent requests served from cache.
- Actual: This query appears on every single request, indicating the cache is not being used.

issue_title: Distribution server sometimes uses wrong pid for started Keycloak server
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

When starting the distribution server the stored pid for the started Keycloak server does not always contain the correct pid.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Should have the correct pid, otherwise it won't recognize the process as managed Keycloak

### Actual behavior

Sometimes uses the wrong pid

### How to Reproduce?

`kc.sh` based differently if a re-augementation is needed or not. It will basically first start Keycloak using `eval java`, which results in the `java` process having a different pid than `kc.sh`. However, if re-augmentation happens then it re-starts the `java` process with `exec java`, which results in the `java` process having the same pid as `kc.sh`.

This results in the first time starting Keycloak with an empty installation it will have the wrong `pid`, or if re-configuring Keycloak resulting in re-agumentation.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Distribution server sometimes uses wrong pid for started Keycloak server

Closes #46110

         at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)

I have traced this to the `profile` scope being present on the client. Here it's failing due to a chain of issues: `updatedAt` is apparently not existing on the user object. The `getAttribute()` call now returns an empty List which gets cast into an empty string and the subsequent type cast to `long` causes the stacktrace above.

Now I wonder why `updatedAt` is missing. Would my custom provider need to set it or is keycloak responsible?
Should the getAttribute function or the type cast be made more resilient to avoid an exception? If the mapper would return it as string, only an empty string would be returned. Only the type casts are causing exceptions. 

### Version

26.3

Login working with no or an empty `updated_at` claim

### Actual behavior

Exception above.

issue_title: [UI Bug] Microsoft/OIDC IdP "Prompt" dropdown saves human-readable label instead of technical value
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug In the Admin Console of Keycloak 26.5.2 and 26.5.3, the "Prompt" field in the Identity Provider configuration incorrectly saves the UI display label to the database. For example, selecting "select_account" results in "Select account" being stored in the identity_provider_config table rather than the OIDC-compliant value select_account. This causes OIDC authorization requests to fail at the provider (e.g., Microsoft Entra ID) due to an invalid parameter. Version: 26.5.3 (and 26.5.2) Environment: - Database: PostgreSQL 18.1 - Deployment: Docker ### Version 26.5.3 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior When a user selects an option from the Prompt dropdown in the Identity Provider settings (e.g., "select_account"), the Admin Console should save the standard OpenID Connect technical string (lowercase, underscore-separated) to the database. For the "select_account" option, the expected value in the identity_provider_config table under the prompt name is select_account. This ensures that the generated authorization URL correctly appends &prompt=select_account, which is a valid parameter for OIDC providers like Microsoft Entra ID. ### Actual behavior The Admin Console incorrectly saves the human-readable UI label instead of the technical value. Selecting "select_account" from the dropdown results in the literal string "Select account" (capitalized with a space) being stored in the identity_provider_config database table. This causes the outbound authorization request to include &prompt=Select+account. Because OIDC providers are case-sensitive and do not recognize the space or capitalization, the login attempt fails with an "invalid parameter" or "bad request" error from the Identity Provider. Furthermore, upon refreshing the Keycloak UI, the field often appears empty because the UI cannot map the "bad" database string back to its own dropdown options. ### How to Reproduce? To Reproduce: 1. Navigate to Identity Providers > Microsoft Login. 2. Open Advanced Settings and locate the Prompt dropdown. 3. Select the option "Select account". 4. Click Save. 5. Inspect the database directly: `SELECT value FROM identity_provider_config WHERE name = 'prompt' AND identity_provider_id = (SELECT internal_id FROM identity_provider WHERE alias = 'microsoft');` 6. Observed Result: The database contains Select account. 7. Expected Result: The database should contain select_account. ### Anything else? **Additional Context**: Manual JSON export/import with the correct lowercase technical value works, but as soon as the UI "Save" button is clicked, the value is overwritten with the incorrect capitalized string.

issue_title: AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie reuse causes cross-user session contamination on re-authentication
commit_message:
Remove root auth session on first offline_access to prevent cross-user session contamination

When a user authenticates with scope=offline_access, the FIRST_OFFLINE_ACCESS path removes
the online user session but not the associated root authentication session. This leaves the
AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie pointing to a still-valid root auth session. If a second user
authenticates from the same browser within the session TTL, Keycloak reuses the stale root
auth session and creates a new user session with the same ID, allowing the second user to
inherit the first user's session.

Remove the root authentication session together with the online user session in the
FIRST_OFFLINE_ACCESS code path, consistent with backchannel logout and the logout endpoint.

Closes #46239

Signed-off-by: Federico Bartoli <federicobartoli95@gmail.com>

issue_title: SPIFFE Identity Provider default TTL too low
issue_body:
### Description

In SPIRE, the default JWT-SVID TTL is 5 minutes ([source](https://spiffe.io/docs/latest/deploying/spire_server/);  `default_jwt_svid_ttl`). This corresponds to the default max value that we have in [FederatedJWTClientValidator](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/51b764b577a6b7cb60f0274c22b36c1bd021347b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/client/FederatedJWTClientValidator.java#L14).

However, it is not uncommon for this do be higher in the real-world deployments. For example, SPIRE Helm chart [sets that to 1h](https://github.com/spiffe/helm-charts-hardened/blob/8abac78a15ecf58d889bea3b3a8333a9ed05abda/charts/spire/charts/spire-server/values.yaml#L960). Therefore, a valid JWT-SVID would fail validation unless you explicitly lower the TTL via Helm values.

Consider the following options:
* set the default max TTL to 3600 in FederatedJWTClientValidator;
* call `validator.setMaximumExpirationTime(3600)` from SpiffeIdentityProvider (similar to [KubernetesIdentityProvider](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/51b764b577a6b7cb60f0274c22b36c1bd021347b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/kubernetes/KubernetesIdentityProvider.java#L34));
* make this configurable via the Admin UI.

### Value Proposition

Using the official Helm chart is a common way to deploy SPIRE. By allowing higher default maximum JWT-SVID TTLs, we will improve interoperability with the real-world SPIFFE/SPIRE deployments.

### Goals

Increase the default maximum JWT-SVID TTL for better interoperability with the real-world SPIFFE/SPIRE deployments

### Non-Goals

—

### Discussion

_No response_

### Notes

_No response_

issue_title: Make sure disabled IDPs are not considered when lookup IDP by issuer
issue_body:
### Description

This task is a follow-up of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/45747 and https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/45453 (which are both addressed right now).

The issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/45747 added some limitations that it is not possible to have multiple IDPs in the realm with same "issuer" as long as some other preconditions are met (EG. 2 IDPs configured to support jwt-authorization grant). But this does not consider if IDP is enabled or not right now.

I assume that we should allow multiple IDPs with same issuer if only one of them is enabled, but others disabled. In that case, we also need to make sure every time when some issuer is going to be switched from disabled to enabled, that there is no other enabled IDP with same "issuer" value (considering that duplicate issuer should not be allowed based on other preconditions, like for example "JWT authorization grant" is used).

If going this path, we should likely fix also `DefaultAlternativeLookupProvider.lookupIdentityProviderFromIssuer` and consider only enabled issuers during lookup.

We should possibly also disable the IDPs with duplicated issuers during migration to this version? For example if someone already have 2 enabled IDPs in their realm with same "Issuer" and with enabled JWT-authorization grant, then they should be rather both disabled during migration. Probably can be done programatically by creating new `MigrateToXY` class in the package `org.keycloak.migration.migrators`

Some relevant discussion points for additional details:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/46024#issuecomment-3880470558
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/46024#issuecomment-3891819371 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/46024#issuecomment-3892259778

issue_title: The RestartLoginCookie does not allow for key rotation as it always uses the active key for verification
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

The verification of the restart cookie uses the current active key, where it instead should use the keys used when it was created. IMHO It is missing a key ID that should be used to decode it. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a2c1055f8df21b53b969f922fb9a044a76d22604/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/RestartLoginCookie.java#L179-L186

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

* When keys are rotated, the cookies should still be decryptable. 
* The cookie should eventually expire for security reasons.

### Actual behavior

* When the keys are rotated, all cookies are invalid as the active key changed
* The cookie (from its cryptographic contents) are valid forever (well, at least until the keys are rotates)


### How to Reproduce?

Review the code

### Anything else?

This might be a blocker for automatic key rotation, see #45091.
commit_message:
Use AESGCM for ecrypting the restart cookie with kid
Closes #46350

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Missing `parentId` in the GroupRepresentation of @keycloak/keycloak-admin-client
issue_body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/46362
Originally posted by **etn406** February 16, 2026 In the TypeScript definition file [groupRepresentation](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/src/defs/groupRepresentation.ts) of the package [@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client), the property `parentId` is missing. It is present in the actual response JSON from Keycloak, as specified in the API documentation [here](https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/latest/rest-api/index.html#GroupRepresentation), so it really is just a typing issue.

Closes #46366

issue_title: CVE-2026-2575 - Denial of Service due to excessive SAMLRequest decompression
issue_body:
### Description

An unauthenticated remote attacker can trigger a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending a highly compressed SAMLRequest via the SAML Redirect Binding. The server fails to enforce size limits during DEFLATE decompression, leading to an OutOfMemoryError (OOM) and process termination.
commit_message:
Limit the inflating size for the SAML redirect binding

Closes #46372

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Unexpected error when logging out with offline session and external IDP
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When a client starts a flow with only offline_access, no SSO session is generated. When the user used an external IDP to authenticate, Keycloak will still try to do a logout at the external IDP if one was used. The logout redirect to the external IDP works but it fails on the `logout_response` endpoint since no session is found.

### Version

26.5.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If the user tries to logout with valid ID token and post_logout_redirect_uri without an SSO session, Keycloak should just redirect back to the post_logout_redirect_uri.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak forwards to the external IDP for logout but fails to handle the logout_response from the external IDP.

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d6320e7-a5f8-421a-b942-b630057d3bda

[docker-compose.yml](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25341109/docker-compose.yml)

[test_setup.tf.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25341123/test_setup.tf.txt)

1. Install terraform (https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/install)
2. Install oauth2c (https://github.com/SecureAuthCorp/oauth2c)
3. Download the two attached files and put them into a directory, remove the `.txt` ending from `test_setup_tf_txt`.
4. Run `docker compose up`
5. Run `terraform init`
6. Run `terraform apply` and confirm with entering `yes`
7. Run: 
`echo "http://localhost:8080/realms/B/protocol/openid-connect/logout?client_id=test&post_logout_redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8803%2Fcallback-logout&id_token_hint=$(oauth2c http://localhost:8080/realms/B \
  --client-id test \
  --grant-type authorization_code \
  --redirect-url http://localhost:8803/callback \
  --auth-method none \
  --response-types code \
  --response-mode query \
  --scopes "openid offline_access" \
  --pkce | grep '{"access_token":"ey' | jq -r .id_token)"`
8. Click "Realm A", login with user `test` password `test`
9. Copy the logout URL from the terminal window and paste it into the browser to trigger the logout.

Please also check the attached video.


### Anything else?

The full related code is quite complex but a related snippet is definitely https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/51010315168f8d59c246b60104ff6b67bb1e322d/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/LogoutEndpoint.java#L413-L417 where Keycloak continues a normal logout process even if an SSO session is not found. This handling was added in this commit: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/88069cd5fb2a30c8a388040b0aebef0b38641604 closing this issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/40398. I am not sure if this was an unintended side effect.
commit_message:
Do not use offline sessions in the logout endpoint
Closes #46379

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: X509 Certificates passed from Traefik PassTlsClientCert middleware broken since 26.5.0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Since 26.5.0, X509 certificate authentication when the certificate is passed via Traefik's PassTLSClientCert middleware is broken. The middleware will pass the client certificate, and an intermediate CA certificate, separated by a comma in the X-Forwarded-Tls-Client-Cert header.

It appears to me that the new Base64 decoding refactor has broken this:

```
Caused by: org.keycloak.common.util.PemException: org.keycloak.common.util.PemException: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Input byte array has incorrect ending byte at 862
	at org.keycloak.common.crypto.PemUtilsProvider.decodeCertificate(PemUtilsProvider.java:60)
	at org.keycloak.common.util.PemUtils.decodeCertificate(PemUtils.java:54)
	at org.keycloak.services.x509.NginxProxySslClientCertificateLookup.decodeCertificateFromPem(NginxProxySslClientCertificateLookup.java:122)
	at org.keycloak.services.x509.AbstractClientCertificateFromHttpHeadersLookup.getCertificateFromHttpHeader(AbstractClientCertificateFromHttpHeadersLookup.java:101)
	... 26 more
Caused by: org.keycloak.common.util.PemException: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Input byte array has incorrect ending byte at 862
	at org.keycloak.common.crypto.PemUtilsProvider.pemToDer(PemUtilsProvider.java:133)
	at org.keycloak.common.crypto.PemUtilsProvider.decodeCertificate(PemUtilsProvider.java:56)
	... 29 more
Caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Input byte array has incorrect ending byte at 862
	at java.base/java.util.Base64$Decoder.decode0(Base64.java:880)
	at java.base/java.util.Base64$Decoder.decode(Base64.java:570)
	at java.base/java.util.Base64$Decoder.decode(Base64.java:593)
	at org.keycloak.common.crypto.PemUtilsProvider.pemToDer(PemUtilsProvider.java:131)
	... 30 more
```

### Version

26.5.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak is able authenticate a user using the X-Forwarded-Tls-Client-Cert as passed by the Traefik PassTLSClientCert middleware.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak fails to authenticate the user.

### How to Reproduce?

Set up Traefik as a reverse proxy with the PassTLSClientCert middleware with `pem: true` configured for it. Configure the x509cert-lookup SPI as documented for Traefik, looking in the X-Forwarded-Tls-Client-Cert header. Set up an authentication flow with an X509/Validate Username Form authenticator.

Access a protected resource with a PKI certificate that contains a full validation chain. 

### Anything else?

_No response_
